name: ChannelsDB_get_channels_pdb
description: Get protein channel, tunnel, and pore data for a PDB structure from ChannelsDB. ChannelsDB is a comprehensive database of channels (tunnels, pores, and voids) found in biomacromolecular structures deposited in the Protein Data Bank. Returns channel geometry profiles (radius, distance along path, 3D coordinates), physicochemical properties (charge, hydrophobicity, polarity, mutability), residue layers lining each channel, and expert annotations when available. Useful for studying transport pathways in membrane proteins, enzyme active site access tunnels, and ion channels. Example: get channels for aquaporin structure 1ymg or hemoglobin 4hhb.

pdb_id.description: PDB structure identifier (4-character code, case-insensitive). Examples: '1ymg' (aquaporin), '3sn6' (beta2-adrenergic receptor-Gs complex), '4hhb' (hemoglobin), '5i6x' (MscL mechanosensitive channel).

oneOf.description: Channel data for the PDB structure

Annotations.description: Expert annotations for channels in this structure

Description.description: Detailed description of the channel function

ReferenceType.description: Type of reference: DOI or PubMed

Channels.description: Detected channels organized by detection method

CSATunnels_MOLE.description: Tunnels detected by MOLE via ChannelsDB 2.0 CSA

CSATunnels_Caver.description: Tunnels detected by CAVER via ChannelsDB 2.0 CSA

CofactorTunnels_MOLE.description: Cofactor access tunnels detected by MOLE

CofactorTunnels_Caver.description: Cofactor access tunnels detected by CAVER

ProcognateTunnels_MOLE.description: Cognate ligand tunnels detected by MOLE

ProcognateTunnels_Caver.description: Cognate ligand tunnels detected by CAVER

name: ChannelsDB_get_channels_cofactor
description: Get cofactor access tunnel data for a PDB structure from ChannelsDB. Specifically detects tunnels that provide access pathways for cofactors and small molecules to reach enzyme active sites or binding pockets. Each channel includes geometric profile data (radius along the path, 3D coordinates), physicochemical properties (charge, hydrophobicity, polarity), and the residue layers that line the tunnel. Use ChannelsDB_get_channels_pdb for a complete view of all channel types including transmembrane pores and reviewed channels.

pdb_id.description: PDB structure identifier (4-character code). Examples: '5i6x' (MscL), '3sn6' (GPCR-Gs complex).

oneOf.description: Channel data focusing on cofactor tunnels

Channels.description: Channel data organized by detection method. Each channel has: Type (Tunnel/Pore), Id, Cavity, Properties (Charge, Hydrophobicity, Polarity, Mutability, NumPositives, NumNegatives), Profile (array of radius/distance/coordinate points along path), and Layers (residues lining the channel).
